			<h4>Banner Materials</h4>

<p>Your banner is made with the finest materials available and printed using state-of-the-art, large-format printers for vibrant colors that pop. All banners are manufactured and printed in the USA.</p>

<p>Exact sizes of banners cannot be guaranteed. Vinyl is a temperature-sensitive material, and some fluctuation can occur due to heat application in printing, finishing, and outside climate. Please note our acceptable size variance is up to 1/2 inch per linear foot (ex., a 5' banner could finish with a 2.5" variance). If your banner needs to be precise, please ask your customer service representative to discuss your specific application.</p>

<div class="row">
<div class="container-flex" style="padding-bottom: .25rem; padding-left: 15px !important; padding-right: 15px !important;">
<h4>Heat Welded Hems & Grommets</h4>

<p>Hems consist of one inch of the vinyl banner material folded over to the backside of the banner and heated together to form a clean and strong one-inch hem. We recommend having hems for all banners to increase the strength and durability of the banner.</p>

<p>You will have the following options when selecting hems and grommets for your banners:</p>

<table border="1" cellpaddi="" cellspaci="">
	<tbody>
		<tr>
			<td style="padding: 5px; width: 50%;"><strong>Top and Bottom Only Hems</strong> - This is our standard finishing option for vinyl banners. The top and bottom of every banner will be heat welded and grommeted.</td>
			<td style="padding: 5px; width: 50%;"><strong>All Four Sides Hems</strong> - This is another common finishing option for vinyl banners. All 4 sides of the banner will be heat welded and grommeted.</td>
		</tr>
		<tr>
			<td><img alt="Top and Bottom Grommets Only Example" src="https://web2printdata.blob.core.windows.net/w2p-cms-1/24-09-04-BANS-Top-and-bottom-hems-and-grommets-only-2.png" style="width: 100%"></td>
			<td><img alt="All 4 Sides Hems & Grommets Example" src="https://web2printdata.blob.core.windows.net/w2p-cms-1/24-09-04-BANS-Top-and-bottom-hems-and-grommets-all-sides.png" style="width: 100%"></td>
		</tr>
	</tbody>
</table>
</div>
</div>

<div class="row">
<div class="container-flex" style="padding-left: 15px !important; padding-right: 15px !important;">
<div style="padding-bottom: .25rem;">
<h4>Pole Pockets</h4>

<p>Pole pockets consist of 2 inches, 3 inches, or 4 inches of material wrapped around the sides, top, or bottom of the banner. The vinyl banner material is folded over the backside of the banner similar to hems, but there is room for a pole or rod to go through the pocket.</p>

<p>You will have the following pole pocket finishing options for your banners:</p>

<ul>
	<li><strong>2" Pole Pockets</strong> - fits a pole 3/4" in diameter</li>
	<li><strong>3" Pole Pockets</strong> - fits a pole 1 1/4" in diameter</li>
	<li><strong>4" Pole Pockets</strong> - fits a pole 1 1/2" in diameter</li>
</ul>

<p>By selecting pole pockets, there will be no increase in the finished size of your banner. Pole pockets can either be 2", 3", or 4". We don't recommend having any text or images within 3 inches of the edge of the banner when you select pole pockets.</p>

<p>**Pole Pockets are not available with reinforced hems or corners. Reinforced corners are not available on banners under 2 feet in height.</p>
</div>

<div style="padding-bottom: .25rem;">
<h4>Reinforced Corners</h4>

<p>Reinforced corners provide extra strength for the banner. The corners have a reinforced hem and are grommeted to each corner of the banner. You cannot have reinforced corners with pole pockets.</p>
</div>

<div style="padding-bottom: .25rem;">
<h4>Reinforced Hems</h4>

<p>Reinforced hems are a 1 3/16" PVC-coated webbing which increases the strength of the banner. The vinyl banner is cut to size, and the reinforcements are heat-welded to the backside of the banner.</p>

<p>We recommend having reinforced hems and reinforced corners on banners over 32 square feet that will be used outdoors or if the banner will be used in a high-wind environment.</p>

<p>**You can not have reinforced hems with pole pockets.</p>
</div>

<div style="padding-bottom: .25rem;">
<h4>Double-Sided Banner Finishing</h4>

<p>We can finish double-sided banners up to 150 square feet. For anything larger, please contact us. Double-sided banners can only have pole pockets or hems and grommet finishing.</p>
</div>

<p>To learn more, visit our<a href="https://www.banners.com/vinyl-banner-finishing-options" rel="noopener" target="_blank"> vinyl banner finishing options</a> page.</p>

<p>**We do not recommend wind slits in banners, therefore, we do not offer them. For more information as to why, visit our <a href="https://www.banners.com/wind-slits">wind slit banners</a> information page.</p>
</div>
</div>

<h2 class="text-center mb-4">More About Mesh Banners <a class="btn btn-sm btn-primary" href="#top">Return to Top</a></h2>

<div class="row p-2 gr-type" id="moreAbout">
<div class="container-fluid">
<p>Mesh Banners are a popular outdoor signage that are manufactured with a strong polyester material that features crisscrossed fiber pattern. This criss cross pattern allows for a 37% airflow through the material itself making them a great option for displaying on fences or high wind areas for outdoor events. </p>

<p>You’ll most commonly see mesh signs displayed on fences at sporting events, construction sites, and storefronts. This is the ideal banner option for these wind susceptible locations because the mesh material design allows air to travel through the banner, thus reducing the chances of it tearing.</p>

<p>Choose from our finishing options including <a href="https://www.banners.com/resources/what-are-reinforced-hems">hems</a> and <a href="https://www.banners.com/resources/what-are-banner-grommets">grommets</a>, hems only, pole pockets, or have the mesh banner cut to the edge. We also offer reinforcement options on hems and corners to increase durability. Adding ball bungees to your order can also help the installation process. Order outdoor mesh banners online today!</p>

<div class="panel">
<div class="panel-body">
<h3 class="text-center" id="ways-to-order">Three Easy Ways to Create Your Mesh Banner Order</h3>

<div class="row p-2 gr-type" id="moreAbout">
<div class="container-fluid">
<h2 class="text-center" id="faqs">Frequently Asked Questions <a class="btn btn-primary btn-sm" href="#top">Return to Top</a></h2>
</div>
</div>

<h3>What material is a mesh banner made of?</h3>

<p>Our mesh banner material is made of 8 oz. PVC polyester with a 70/30 vinyl-to-hole ratio, creating approximately 37% airflow.</p>

<h3>How large or how small can my mesh banner be?</h3>

<p>Mesh banners can be ordered as small as 1' × 1' and as large as 50' × 16'.</p>

<h3>What are the benefits of using mesh banners versus vinyl banners?</h3>

<p>Mesh banners are designed to reduce wind resistance by allowing airflow through the material, which helps prevent tearing. Vinyl banners are better for maximum color vibrancy and fine detail.</p>

<h3>Are mesh banners good for windy locations?</h3>

<p>Yes. Mesh banners are ideal for high-wind environments such as fences, scaffolding, and outdoor events because they reduce wind load.</p>

<h3>Do mesh banners look see-through?</h3>

<p>Up close, the holes are visible. From a normal viewing distance, the printed design appears clear while still allowing airflow and some light to pass through.</p>

<h3>Will my design lose detail when printed on mesh?</h3>

<p>Some vibrance and fine detail may be reduced due to the mesh material. Approximately 30% of detail is lost, so larger text and bold graphics work best.</p>

<h3>What size text works best on a mesh banner?</h3>

<p>Larger, bolder fonts are recommended. Thin lines and very small text may lose clarity compared to vinyl banners.</p>

<h3>Are mesh banners printed in full color?</h3>

<p>Yes, all mesh banners are printed in full CMYK color.</p>

<h3>Can I order double-sided mesh banners?</h3>

<p>No. Mesh banners are printed on one side only. Double-sided printing is available for select vinyl banner options.</p>

<h3>What finishing option is best for mesh fence banners?</h3>

<p>Reinforced hems with grommets are the most common choice. They help distribute tension evenly and improve durability outdoors.</p>

<h3>How far apart should grommets be for outdoor mesh banners?</h3>

<p>Standard grommet placement is every 2–3 feet along the edges, with additional grommets at the corners for stability.</p>

<h3>What is the best way to hang a mesh banner?</h3>

<p>Ball bungees are recommended because they provide flexibility and even tension. Rope can work but may cause stress if overtightened. Zip ties are not recommended.</p>

<h3>Can mesh banners be used in rain and sun?</h3>

<p>Yes. Mesh banners are designed for outdoor use. Proper installation helps extend their lifespan in harsh conditions.</p>

<h3>How long do mesh banners typically last?</h3>

<p>With proper installation and storage, mesh banners typically last up to 2 years.</p>

<h3>What file type and resolution should I upload for best print quality?</h3>

<p>Vector files are ideal. For raster artwork, upload a high-resolution file at the final print size or larger for the best results.</p>
